# Implementation Plan: Auto-populate Lesson Blocks
## Overview
When an exercise or content page is created/updated/deleted with a lesson reference, automatically sync that lesson's `blocks` textarea (JSON array). Remove manual add/delete buttons from the admin UI. Include a one-time migration for existing data.

task.md.L2:
# Auto-populate lesson blocks from related content pages and exercises

### Category

feature - Feature functionality

### Feature Area

lessons, admin

### Scenario

Currently, lesson blocks (exercises, content pages) must be manually added to a lesson via "Add Exercise" / "Add Content Page" buttons in the LessonBlocksField. Since exercises and content pages already have a lesson relationship field pointing to their parent lesson, this manual step is redundant.

Desired behavior:

When an exercise or content page is created/published with a lesson reference, it should automatically appear in that lesson's blocks array.
When an exercise or content page is deleted or its lesson reference changes, it should be automatically removed from the old lesson's blocks.
Block ordering should still be editable via drag-and-drop.
UI changes after implementation:

Remove the "+ Add Exercise" and "+ Content Page" buttons from LessonBlocksField
Remove the delete (trash) button from each lesson block row
Keep: drag-and-drop reordering, move up/down buttons

### Prototype Reference

_No response_

### Test Fixture

_No response_

### Site Behaviors

_No response_

### Design System Components

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_

task.json.L0: Title: Auto-populate lesson blocks from exercises and content pages | Type: feature | Risk: medium
task.json.L1:
- task_type: feature
- title: Auto-populate lesson blocks from exercises and content pages
- description: Automatically populate lesson blocks (exercises, content pages) based on their lesson relationship field. When an exercise or content page is created/published with a lesson reference, it automatically appears in that lesson's blocks array. When deleted or lesson reference changes, it's automatically removed. Remove manual "Add Exercise" and "Add Content Page" buttons, remove delete buttons from block rows, keep drag-and-drop reordering. Run one-time migration to populate all existing lessons.
- scope: [4 items] src/ui/admin/LessonBlocksField/index.tsx, src/server/payload/collections/Exercises/index.ts, src/server/payload/collections/ContentPages.ts
- risk_level: medium
- questions: [1 items] During the one-time migration, if a lesson already has manually-ordered blocks, should we append missing exercises/content pages to the end, or regenerate the entire blocks array from scratch based on creation order?

```json
{
  "task_type": "feature",
  "title": "Auto-populate lesson blocks from exercises and content pages",
  "description": "Automatically populate lesson blocks (exercises, content pages) based on their lesson relationship field. When an exercise or content page is created/published with a lesson reference, it automatically appears in that lesson's blocks array. When deleted or lesson reference changes, it's automatically removed. Remove manual \"Add Exercise\" and \"Add Content Page\" buttons, remove delete buttons from block rows, keep drag-and-drop reordering. Run one-time migration to populate all existing lessons.",
  "scope": [
    "src/ui/admin/LessonBlocksField/index.tsx",
    "src/server/payload/collections/Exercises/index.ts",
    "src/server/payload/collections/ContentPages.ts",
    "scripts/migrate-lesson-blocks.ts"
  ],
  "risk_level": "medium",
  "questions": [
    "During the one-time migration, if a lesson already has manually-ordered blocks, should we append missing exercises/content pages to the end, or regenerate the entire blocks array from scratch based on creation order?"
  ]
}
```
